strategies.name: Wing Skip & Corner Attack
strategies.summary: Skip ball to weak-side wing, collapse zone, attack corner.
description: Skip the ball to the weak-side wing to force both top defenders to rotate. The bottom corner defender cannot cover both the wing and the corner simultaneously — one will be open.
When to use: Zone is sitting deep with no ball pressure. Corner shooter has a mismatch on the bottom defender. Ball handler is patient enough to hold and wait for the skip.
When not to use: Corner shooter is cold or being denied hard. Defense is playing a scramble zone with active hands in passing lanes.
What it gives you: Open corner 3. If the defense overrotates, the high post becomes available for a mid or dump.

strategies.name: High Post Split
strategies.summary: Feed the high post elbow to split the two top defenders.
description: Pass into the high post elbow. The two top zone defenders must decide who covers — this creates a gap between them. The high post player reads the defense: shoot the mid if they sag, drive the gap if they close hard, or dump to a cutter coming weak side.
When to use: Your big can catch and face up from the elbow. Top defenders are playing too far apart or too close. Cutters are ready on both sides of the lane.
When not to use: High post player cannot shoot the mid — defense will sag and clog the lane entirely.
What it gives you: Elbow mid, drive and dump to a cutting big, or kick-out 3 if the defense collapses on the drive.

strategies.name: Short Corner Overload
strategies.summary: Stack wing and short corner on same side to overload one zone defender.
description: Place a player at the short corner (baseline extended, inside the arc) and a player at the wing on the same side. The bottom zone defender cannot guard both. Quick ball movement between the two forces the defense to choose — one is always open.
When to use: Bottom zone defender is slow to rotate. Post player can score from short corner.
When not to use: Defense shifts to match your overload.
What it gives you: Short corner mid or lob to post.

strategies.name: Pin Down Screen
strategies.summary: Downscreen on baseline to free a shooter cutting to the wing.
description: A big sets a downscreen on the baseline for a guard cutting up to the wing. The screened defender must choose — fight over the screen and give up the catch-and-shoot, or go under and give up the open 3. If they fight over, the cutter can curl to the basket instead.
When to use: Shooter's defender is playing tight and denying. Screener's defender tends to help on cutters, leaving the screener open on the pop.
When not to use: Defense is switching all screens — look for mismatches instead.
What it gives you: Open wing 3 for the shooter. Curl layup if the defender goes under. Pop-out mid for the screener if their defender helps.

strategies.name: Transition Early Pass
strategies.summary: Push pace and hit the early pass before defense sets.
description: On a made basket or rebound, immediately push the ball up the floor and look for the early pass to a wing sprinting ahead of their defender. The advantage window closes within 2-3 seconds — if the early pass is not there, pull up and set your offense. Do not force it.
When to use: Defense is slow getting back after a made basket. You have a fast wing who can beat their defender down the floor.
When not to use: Protecting a lead late in the game. The defense has a dedicated safety who always gets back first.
What it gives you: Layup or open mid before the defense sets. 2v1 or 3v2 advantage if pushed quickly.

strategies.name: Ball Reversal vs Press
strategies.summary: Reverse the ball quickly to attack before the press can reset.
description: Against a full court press, resist the instinct to dribble through it. Use quick passes to reverse the ball. The press is designed to trap on one side — reversing forces the entire press to rotate, creating open lanes in the middle of the floor.
When to use: Defense is running a trapping press that overloads one side. Players are calm and not panicking under pressure.
When not to use: Players are making careless passes — take care of the ball first before trying to attack.
What it gives you: Open lane in the middle of the floor. Easy layup if the press overcommits. Free throws if the defense fouls to stop the break.
